name: Cascada de Artazul - circular from Ultzurrun
name_original: Cascada de Artazul – circular desde Ultzurrun
region_intro: Jogging routes around Ollo offer access to varied terrain in the surrounding areas. While Ollo itself is primarily a residential and urban area, the nearby region provides opportunities for outdoor running. These routes often feature natural elements such as waterfalls, hills, and panoramic viewpoints, allowing runners to explore diverse landscapes. The trails cater to different fitness levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Ollo The most popular jogging route is Cascada de Artazul - circular from Ultzurrun, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 55 minutes to complete. This route features a waterfall and offers a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local runners is Txurregi Summit (1,121 m) loop from Errotz, a difficult 7.9 miles (12.8 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading to the summit of Txurregi. Local runners also love the Gaztelu – Txurregi loop from Errotz, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Jogging around Ollo is defined by waterfalls, hills, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Ollo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Ollo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Ollo?
A: There are over 65 running routes available around Ollo, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a wide selection, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails near Ollo?
A: The running routes around Ollo offer diverse terrain, often featuring natural elements such as waterfalls, hills, and panoramic viewpoints. You can expect a mix of paths that allow you to explore varied landscapes.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in the Ollo area?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy running route available around Ollo, perfect for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails around Ollo?
A: The routes around Ollo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Over 500 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic viewpoints and natural features.

Q: Are there any running routes around Ollo that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can definitely find routes with waterfalls. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971581412/cascada-de-artazul-circular-from-ultzurrun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Artazul - circular from Ultzurrun&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.7-mile (7.5 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete and features the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artazul Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes with panoramic views near Ollo?
A: Absolutely! Many trails offer stunning vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Begiratoki panoramikoa loop from Aizpun&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.3 km) path specifically known for its panoramic views and some elevation gain.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced runners will find plenty of challenges. There are 44 difficult routes around Ollo, including trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaztelu loop from Irurtzun&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.7-mile (12.4 km) trail through hilly terrain, often completed in about 2 hours and 7 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Ollo area?
A: Many of the running routes around Ollo are circular, offering convenient loops. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971581412/cascada-de-artazul-circular-from-ultzurrun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Artazul - circular from Ultzurrun&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24644899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaztelu – Txurregi loop from Errotz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks can I see while running near Ollo?
A: Beyond waterfalls and viewpoints, the region around Ollo is home to several natural monuments and summits. You might encounter features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3065525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portupekoleze Arch&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3379347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beriain Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain passes accessible from running routes around Ollo?
A: Yes, the area features several mountain passes that can be part of longer, more challenging running adventures. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2043845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arteta (Alto de Goñi)&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated terrain and expansive views.

Q: Are the running trails around Ollo suitable for dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for each route are not always listed, many natural trails in such regions are generally suitable for well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations for specific trails and be mindful of wildlife and other users.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Ollo?
A: Given the varied terrain with hills and waterfalls, spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery for running around Ollo. Summers can be warm, and winters might bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations.
tips:
Beautiful 40m waterfall
Path to the cascade can be flooded. If you want to go higher from the cascade, the terrain may be muddy and slippery!
